shortDescription: Dept of Home Affairs · Subclass 500 · CoE required · Genuine Student (GS) requirement

policyAlert.summary: Subclass 500 visa charge is from AUD $2,000 (since 1 Jul 2025) and the living-cost figure is AUD $29,710/yr for a single applicant — both indexed periodically. Offshore applications are prioritised under Ministerial Direction No. 111. Verify all fees, financial-capacity amounts and processing times on homeaffairs.gov.au before quoting; treat these as live CRM/official values.

alert.title: Genuine Student (GS) requirement
alert.body: The GS requirement replaced the GTE test (for applications lodged on/after 23 Mar 2024). It is assessed through targeted questions inside the ImmiAccount application form (150-word limit per answer) plus supporting evidence — Home Affairs prefers applicants answer the form questions rather than attach a separate statement, and gives more weight to evidence-backed answers. Future PR intentions do NOT count against the applicant under GS. A generic or unevidenced response, or a course that doesn't fit the applicant's background, is a leading refusal trigger — especially for higher evidence-level (EL3) cohorts such as India, Nepal, Bangladesh and Bhutan.

kpis.label: Processing time
kpis.sub: By sector, country & EL; MD 111 priority — verify live tool (dynamic)

kpis.label: Our approval rate
kpis.sub: Pull from live CRM case data; not a prediction of any individual outcome

about.label: Description
about.value: Subclass 500 student visa for full-time study at an Australian CRICOS-registered institution. Requires Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) and online ImmiAccount application.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid CoE from a CRICOS-registered provider · Genuine Student (GS) requirement · Financial capacity (12 months' living costs + tuition + travel) · English proficiency for the course level · OSHC for the full visa period · Health and character requirements

about.label: Financial evidence
about.value: Show access to 12 months' living costs (AUD $29,710 for a single applicant, since 10 May 2024) PLUS 12 months' tuition (course fee for the first year) PLUS travel, OR meet the annual-income alternative (parent/partner income, official tax evidence). Funds must be genuinely available; verify the current amounts on homeaffairs.gov.au.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Maintain enrolment and satisfactory course progress, keep OSHC for the whole stay, notify the provider of your address within 7 days of arrival, and comply with work limits (48 hours per fortnight in study periods). A Subclass 485 Temporary Graduate visa may follow eligible study.

eligibility.note: Answered via targeted ImmiAccount form questions (150-word limit each) with supporting evidence; a separate statement is not preferred. Cover ties, course/provider rationale and course benefit. Future PR intent does not count against the applicant.
12 months' living costs (AUD $29,710 single) + first-year tuition + travel, or the annual-income alternative; funds must be genuinely available and source-documented.
IELTS/PTE/TOEFL/OET per provider and course level; packaged ELICOS may apply; check the provider's and Home Affairs' thresholds.

redFlagsBanner: After refusal, analyse refusal letter and strengthen GS statement and financial evidence before reapplication with new CoE.

redFlags.title: Weak Genuine Student statement
redFlags.description: Generic GS, course mismatch with background, unclear career plan.
redFlags.fix: Course-specific GS linking prior study/work to Australia program

redFlags.title: Insufficient financial evidence
redFlags.description: Unseasoned funds or amount below Home Affairs threshold.
redFlags.fix: 12-month funds per guidelines; sponsor ITR; loan evidence

redFlags.title: CoE / provider issues
redFlags.description: Non-CRICOS provider or course start passed.
redFlags.fix: Verify CRICOS register before CoE fee

redFlags.title: OSHC gaps
redFlags.fix: Purchase OSHC covering full visa period

redFlags.title: Prior refusals not addressed
redFlags.description: Hidden Australian or other-country refusals, or any false statement. Non-disclosure risks refusal under PIC 4020 and a potential 3-year exclusion.
redFlags.fix: Disclose every prior refusal and explain changed circumstances; never conceal history (PIC 4020 risk)

redFlags.title: English scores below requirement
redFlags.description: Test expired or below course minimum.
redFlags.fix: Retake and obtain valid scores before apply

redFlags.title: Course/credential mismatch or unexplained 'step-down'
redFlags.description: Choosing a course below or unrelated to the applicant's existing qualifications without a clear rationale — a core GS failure point.
redFlags.fix: Justify the course choice and career logic explicitly in the GS statement; align level and field to the applicant's history

redFlags.title: Funds not genuinely available / unexplained large deposit
redFlags.description: Amount is present but unseasoned or its source is undocumented (recent lump sum), failing the genuine-access test.
redFlags.fix: Show 3+ months' history, document the source (sale, loan sanction, savings, sponsor income), and evidence genuine access

faqs.a: No. The Department of Home Affairs assesses the Genuine Student requirement, financial capacity, English, health and character. A strong, honest file improves the chances but a grant is never guaranteed — and we never promise one.

The Genuine Student (GS) requirement (which replaced GTE for applications lodged on/after 23 Mar 2024) tests whether the applicant genuinely intends to study. It is assessed through targeted questions inside the ImmiAccount application form — each answer capped at 150 words — plus supporting evidence. Home Affairs prefers applicants answer the form questions rather than attach a separate GS statement, and gives more weight to evidence-backed answers. Importantly, intending to seek PR later does NOT count against the applicant. A generic or unevidenced response is a leading refusal cause.

Show 12 months' living costs — AUD $29,710 for a single applicant (since 10 May 2024) — PLUS the first 12 months' tuition PLUS travel, with more for any accompanying family. An income alternative exists (parent/partner annual income evidenced by official tax documents). Funds must be genuinely available and source-documented. Verify the current figures on homeaffairs.gov.au.

Yes. A partner can be included in the application or apply later as a subsequent entrant; they must be a genuine spouse/de facto partner and the relationship must be evidenced. Each adult family member attracts an additional visa charge. A partner's work rights flow from the student's stage of study. Assess case-by-case.

Yes — up to 48 hours per fortnight during study periods and unlimited hours during scheduled course breaks. Work cannot start before the course begins, and the student must maintain enrolment, attendance, course progress and OSHC. Verify the current condition on homeaffairs.gov.au, as work rules change.

After the CoE is issued, and with enough lead time before the course start for processing, biometrics (if required), health checks and any outstanding evidence. Check the live processing-time tool and the provider accepted start dates; offshore files are prioritised under Ministerial Direction No. 111.

Often. A health examination by a panel physician is required depending on country of residence, intended study (e.g. healthcare/teaching), and length of stay. Book early — it can delay the grant. Follow the exact instructions generated in ImmiAccount.

Eligible graduates may apply for a Subclass 485 Temporary Graduate visa to gain post-study work experience. Note the 485 charge rose substantially in 2026 — verify the current fee and eligibility (including any field/qualification and age limits) on homeaffairs.gov.au before advising.

faqs.q: How much is the Subclass 500 visa application charge?
faqs.a: From AUD $2,000 per applicant since 1 July 2025 (up from $1,600), payable in ImmiAccount at lodgement and non-refundable even if refused. A partner aged 18+ and dependent children attract additional charges. Lower charges apply to eligible Pacific Island and Timor-Leste applicants. Always confirm the current charge on homeaffairs.gov.au before quoting.

faqs.q: What is OSHC and is it mandatory?
faqs.a: Overseas Student Health Cover is mandatory for the entire visa period and must be arranged before the visa is granted. Cost varies by provider and duration (roughly AUD $500–$800/yr for a single student — verify with the provider). The policy start/end dates must cover the full visa, not just the course.

faqs.q: What is Ministerial Direction No. 111 and why does it matter?
faqs.a: MD 111 sets the priority order in which Home Affairs processes offshore student visa applications, linked to providers' enrolment allocations. It can materially affect processing speed, so the provider and the applicant's place in the allocation matter. Always quote the live processing-time guidance rather than a fixed number.

There is no single number — it depends on the course and provider, who set the accepted tests (IELTS, PTE Academic, TOEFL iBT, OET, etc.) and minimum scores. Packaged ELICOS can lower the direct-entry threshold. Confirm both the provider's and Home Affairs' requirements, and check the test validity date.

faqs.q: What does the financial 'genuine access' test mean?
faqs.a: It is not enough to show a balance — the funds must be genuinely available to the applicant or sponsor. A large unexplained deposit just before lodging is a common refusal trigger. Show several months of history and document the source (savings, sale, sanctioned education loan, or evidenced sponsor income).

faqs.q: Can a refused Subclass 500 be appealed?
faqs.a: Onshore refusals may have AAT/ART merits review rights; offshore refusals generally do not and require a corrected fresh application with a new CoE. Read the refusal grounds carefully (commonly GS, financial capacity, or PIC 4020) and fix the specific issue before reapplying. Never re-lodge the same weak file.

faqs.q: Does field of study or institution affect the application?
faqs.a: Yes. The provider must be CRICOS-registered, and the evidence level (EL) applied to the applicant's country and provider affects how much documentation is scrutinised. India, Nepal, Bangladesh and Bhutan are at the higher EL3 setting, so build a thorough GS statement and financial pack.

faqs.q: When should the application be lodged relative to course start?
faqs.a: After the CoE is issued and with enough lead time for processing, biometrics (if required), health checks and any English/financial evidence. Allow several weeks to a few months; check the live processing-time tool and the provider's accepted start dates.

faqs.q: What relationship/financial evidence is strongest for a sponsor?
faqs.a: Where parents fund the study: their bank statements with history, official income/tax documents, a sponsorship/affidavit of support, and relationship proof. For loans: a sanction letter from a recognised lender covering tuition and living. The source matters as much as the amount.

Nothing about the outcome — the Department of Home Affairs decides. FLC prepares a strong, honest, well-documented file with a credible GS statement and verified funds, and never promises a grant, in writing or verbally.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, visa, OSHC, and medical costs
CoE must be from CRICOS-registered provider only
proTips: Purchase OSHC for exact visa period
Write the GS statement per client — name the course, provider, career outcome and home ties; never reuse a template.
Confirm the CRICOS registration of the provider AND course before any deposit.
Document genuine access to funds (3+ months history + source); a last-minute lump sum is a leading refusal trigger.
Treat the AUD $2,000 charge, AUD $29,710 living figure and processing times as live values — verify on homeaffairs.gov.au and pull from current CRM data.
postApproval: Send visa grant notification and conditions
Remind OSHC maintenance and enrolment deadlines

performance.note: Approval rate and file counts are indicative and should be pulled live from CRM case data, not published as fixed figures. Verify before release.

timeline.title: Home Affairs processing — varies by sector/country/EL (MD 111); quote the live tool

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Consistency fix + version alignment: removed invented statistics that remained from earlier passes — set performance.ourRate/industryRate to null, converted hardcoded file counts (192/165/19/8) and the approvalFactors ours/benchmark pairs to {{VERIFY_LIVE}}/null, and replaced the hardcoded approval-rate KPI (86%) with {{VERIFY_LIVE}}. Brings Australia in line with the rest of the service-library (no published approval rates or unsourced benchmarks). No other content changed; all factual content and prior corrections are unchanged.

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Publication-readiness fixes: removed the fixed 4–8 week processing claim (now 'varies — verify live tool', consistent with MD 111 and the file's own warnings); aligned Genuine Student content to the official model (answered via 150-word ImmiAccount form questions with evidence, separate statement not preferred, future PR intent does not count against the applicant); corrected the GS resource URL to /genuine-student-requirement; made the financial-increase date precise (10 May 2024); and flagged the approval rate, file counts and benchmark factors as live CRM values rather than fixed published figures.

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Gold-standard content pass: replaced auto-generated template FAQs and quiz questions with real counsellor Q&A; corrected the Subclass 500 charge to from AUD $2,000 (since 1 Jul 2025) and the living-cost figure to AUD $29,710 (single, since 10 May 2024); added Genuine Student detail, Ministerial Direction No. 111, EL3 cohort note, PIC 4020 and genuine-access guidance; removed wrong-domain leftovers (GIC/blocked account/CAS) from the cost breakdown; flagged fees, living costs, processing times and approval rate as values that should be dynamic/CRM-sourced; varied the quiz answer key across positions. Also replaced residual GTE terminology and the GTE resource link with the current Genuine Student (GS) references.

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Expanded FAQs to 30 counselor Q&A pairs.

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Expanded quiz to 75 levelled questions (25 per level).

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Subclass 500 counselor content with GS and 2026 fee notes.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: GS statement must be original—template GS is top QA rejection internally.

resources.title: Home Affairs — Genuine Student (GS) requirement
resources.description: Official guidance on the Genuine Student requirement (replaced GTE); verify current GS questions and evidence expectations

quiz.question: Mandatory enrolment document?
quiz.explanation: CoE required for Subclass 500.

quiz.question: GS requirement assesses:
options: Genuine intent to study and comply with visa
quiz.explanation: Genuine Student framework.

quiz.question: OSHC is:
quiz.explanation: Required health cover for students.

quiz.question: Top refusal factor often:
quiz.explanation: GS and credibility key.

quiz.question: Counselors must never:
quiz.explanation: No guarantees permitted.

quiz.question: Which visa lets international students study full-time in Australia?
quiz.explanation: The Subclass 500 is the primary student visa.

quiz.question: What document confirms enrolment for a Subclass 500 application?
options: Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) from a CRICOS provider
quiz.explanation: Australia uses a CoE from a CRICOS-registered provider (CAS=UK, I-20=US, PAL=Canada).

quiz.question: The Genuine Student (GS) requirement replaced which former test?
quiz.explanation: GS replaced GTE as the genuineness test.

quiz.question: From 1 July 2025, the Subclass 500 visa charge is from:
quiz.explanation: The base charge rose to from AUD $2,000 on 1 Jul 2025.

quiz.question: The single-applicant living-cost figure (since 10 May 2024) is about:
quiz.explanation: The living-cost figure rose to AUD $29,710 for a single applicant.

quiz.question: Subclass 500 students may work how many hours during study periods?
quiz.explanation: The cap is 48 hours per fortnight in study periods; unlimited in scheduled breaks.

quiz.question: Health cover required for the full visa period is called:
quiz.explanation: OSHC is mandatory for the whole visa period.

quiz.question: Where is the Subclass 500 application lodged?
quiz.explanation: Applications are lodged online in ImmiAccount.

quiz.question: What does the financial 'genuine access' test require?
options: Funds genuinely available and source-documented, not just a balance
quiz.explanation: Funds must be genuinely available with a documented source; lump sums are scrutinised.

quiz.question: Which direction governs the priority order for offshore student processing?
quiz.explanation: MD 111 sets offshore processing priority tied to provider allocations.

quiz.question: Non-disclosure of a prior refusal can trigger refusal under:
quiz.explanation: PIC 4020 covers false or misleading information; exclusion can follow.

quiz.question: India, Nepal, Bangladesh and Bhutan are currently at which evidence level?
quiz.explanation: These countries are set at EL3, so documentation is scrutinised more.

quiz.question: The annual-income alternative to savings must be evidenced by:
options: Official government/tax documentation of parent or partner income
quiz.explanation: The income alternative requires official tax evidence of the sponsor's income.

quiz.question: School-aged dependent children add an annual schooling cost of about:
quiz.explanation: Official schooling cost is AUD $13,502 per child per year (additional to living costs).

quiz.question: When can a Subclass 500 holder begin working?
options: Only on/after the course start date
quiz.explanation: Work cannot begin before the course starts.

quiz.question: OSHC dates must cover:
options: The full visa period, not just the course
quiz.explanation: OSHC must cover the entire visa period and be in place before grant.

quiz.question: A 32-year-old MBA holder applies for a 1-year diploma in a new field. Main risk?
options: A GS/course-mismatch concern — justify the choice and career logic
quiz.explanation: Step-down or field-change without rationale is a core GS failure.

quiz.question: A bank statement shows a large deposit 4 days before lodging, no history. Best action?
options: Document the source and show seasoning before lodging
quiz.explanation: Genuine-access test: source and seasoning matter, not just the amount.

quiz.question: An offshore Subclass 500 is refused. Typical recourse?
options: A corrected fresh application with a new CoE (offshore generally has no merits review)
quiz.explanation: Offshore refusals usually require a corrected re-application; onshore may have ART review.

quiz.question: A client asks for a written guarantee of the visa. You:
options: Refuse and use approved non-guaranteeing language
quiz.explanation: Never guarantee an outcome; Home Affairs decides.

quiz.question: A counsellor quotes the old AUD $710 charge. The issue?
options: Outdated — it is from AUD $2,000 since 1 Jul 2025; verify live
quiz.explanation: The charge rose to from AUD $2,000; always verify on homeaffairs.gov.au.

quiz.question: A client omits a prior UK student-visa refusal on the form. The risk?
options: PIC 4020 misrepresentation — refusal and a potential 3-year exclusion
quiz.explanation: Undisclosed refusals risk PIC 4020 consequences.

quiz.question: The single strongest lever on a borderline GS file is:
options: A specific, coherent GS statement with course-career logic and home ties
quiz.explanation: A genuine, specific GS narrative is the key lever.

quiz.question: A student wants to work 60 hours in a fortnight during term. Advise:
options: Not allowed — the cap is 48 hours per fortnight in study periods
quiz.explanation: Exceeding 48 hours per fortnight in study periods can risk the visa.

dos: Verify CRICOS and CoE before apply
Show funds per Home Affairs amounts
Match the course level/field to the applicant's background and state the rationale in the GS
Verify CRICOS registration and OSHC dates before lodging
donts: Do not apply with expired CoE
Do not promise a grant or a fixed processing time — MD 111 priority varies
Do not lodge unseasoned or unexplained funds — the genuine-access test will fail
mistakes: Course unrelated to background without explanation
Quoting the old AUD $710 / $1,600 charge — it is from AUD $2,000 since 1 Jul 2025
Reusing a templated GS statement across clients (a core refusal cause)

sampleDocs.title: Sample financial capacity proof (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Funds for tuition + travel + 12 months living.

sampleDocs.title: Sample academic transcripts (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Qualification history for Genuine Student (GS) course alignment.

applicant.summary: Subclass 500 students may work up to 48 hours per fortnight during study periods and unlimited hours during scheduled course breaks. Work cannot start before the course begins.
details: 48 hours per fortnight is the cap during study periods (a fortnight is any 14-day period); unlimited during scheduled breaks.
Must maintain enrolment, attendance, course progress and OSHC.
Some courses (e.g. certain research/aged-care roles) have specific exceptions — verify the live condition.
restrictions: Work must not begin before the course start date
Breaching the 48-hour cap can risk the visa

spouse.summary: A student's partner included on the visa generally has work rights aligned to the student's study stage; for postgraduate/coursework-by-research the partner may have unrestricted work — verify the current condition on homeaffairs.gov.au.

fullCostBreakdown.title: Full cost breakdown — Australia – Student Visa (Subclass 500)
fullCostBreakdown.disclaimer: Indicative costs for counsellor discussions only. The visa charge and living-cost figure are from official Home Affairs sources and should be re-verified on homeaffairs.gov.au before quoting — they are indexed periodically. INR equivalents move with exchange rates.

items.label: Subclass 500 visa application charge (primary)
items.notes: From 1 Jul 2025 (was $1,600). Non-refundable. Partner 18+ also pays; child under 18 less. Verify on homeaffairs.gov.au.

items.label: Biometrics (if required)
items.notes: Some nationalities only; collected at an AVAC

items.label: Health examination (panel physician)
items.notes: Paid to the physician, not Home Affairs; varies by country

items.label: Tuition deposit already paid
items.notes: Use the CoE tuition amount; deduct any prepaid amount from required funds

items.label: Living costs (Home Affairs, single applicant)
items.notes: Since May 2024 (was $24,505). Add for partner/children. Verify current figure.

items.label: OSHC
items.notes: Mandatory for the full visa period; varies by provider

totals.label: Indicative first-year budget (excl. tuition band)
totals.value: Counselor to calculate from sections above
totals.notes: Always cross-check the CoE / Offer of Place and official Home Affairs fee pages before client commitment.
